texto: Fears and expectations that date back to earlier experiences of dependency, but that didn't arise during courtship or dating, are activated as commitment to the relationship increases. As a result, partners start to anticipate the worst, not the best from their relationship.
explicacao: Citação de Stan Tatkin sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. “Fears and expectations that date back to earlier experiences of dependency, but that didn't arise…” articula amor, commitment, dating em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como termino e dating.

frase_original: Fears and expectations that date back to earlier experiences of dependency, but that didn't arise during courtship or dating, are activated as commitment to the relationship increases. As a result, partners start to anticipate the worst, not the best from their relationship.

texto: The couple bubble is an agreement to put the relationship before anything and everything else. It means putting your partner's well-being, self-esteem and distress relief first. And it means your partner does the same for you. You both agree to do it for each other. Therefore, you say to each other, "We come first." In this way, you cement your relationship. It is like making a pact or taking a vow, or like reinforcing a vow you already took with one another.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Stan Tatkin: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a autoestima. O trecho “The couple bubble is an agreement to put the relationship before anything and everything else. It…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, love, marriage pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de casamento e marriage.

frase_original: The couple bubble is an agreement to put the relationship before anything and everything else. It means putting your partner's well-being, self-esteem and distress relief first. And it means your partner does the same for you. You both agree to do it for each other. Therefore, you say to each other, "We come first." In this way, you cement your relationship. It is like making a pact or taking a vow, or like reinforcing a vow you already took with one another.

texto: When we enter into a relationship, we want to matter to our partner, to be visible and important....We want to know our efforts are noticed and appreciated. We want to know our relationship is regarded as important by our partner and will not be relegated to second or third place because of a competing person, task, or thing.
explicacao: Citação de Stan Tatkin sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “When we enter into a relationship, we want to matter to our partner, to be visible and important.…” articula reflexao, marriage, relationships em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como casamento e relationships.

texto: Devote yourself to your partner's sense of safety and security and not simply to your idea about what that should be. What may make you feel safe and secure may not be what your partner requires from you. Your job is to know what matters to your partner and how to make him or her feel safe and secure.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Stan Tatkin sobre temas ligados a amor. A afirmação “Devote yourself to your partner's sense of safety and security and not simply to your idea about …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, love, marriage, útil em momentos de casamento e marriage.
